description: Decision pack for git history exposure review. Recommends a clean public snapshot for any future public GitHub developer-preview release. Full internal history publication not recommended without explicit forensic audit approval.
recommendation: Publish a clean public snapshot. Use an orphan branch or new public repository initialized from the current vetted working tree. Do not include internal git history in the initial public release. Full history publication may be revisited only if a later dedicated forensic history audit produces explicit evidence that the full history is clean.
gl159_requirements: Create a private snapshot branch or private mirror repository from current vetted working tree
Verify resulting tree matches expected content
Run GL-157 scan gate against snapshot tree and confirm exit 0
Run all public-readiness validation tests against the snapshot
Verify git remote configuration shows no accidental GitHub remote
Verify .claude/ is not present in the snapshot tree
Verify no private hostnames or internal paths in snapshot tree
Verify no accidental GitHub publication occurs during dry run
Document dry-run result in a structured report artifact
gl160_go_no_go_inputs: Whether to publish clean snapshot to public GitHub
Whether repository metadata is correct for developer preview
Whether required release caveats are present in README and SECURITY
Whether GL-159 dry-run result approves publication
Whether scan gate passes on final snapshot before publication
Whether any new issues from GL-159 must be resolved first
next_issue: GL-159 GitHub Private Mirror Dry Run
